_meta.description: Execution policy IR — encodes execution.contract.md §§2–6 as a structured intermediate representation
_meta.note: Raw contract files remain canonical source of truth. This policy file is a read-only cache/IR and never overrides the source.

flags.edges_and_forbidden_dual_spec: edges defines all allowed transitions; forbidden explicitly lists transitions most likely to be confused or mistakenly attempted. Every valid path must appear in edges. Every entry in forbidden is also absent from edges (implied disallowed) but is declared explicitly because it is a common source of confusion or an enforcement invariant. NOTE: The wildcard forbidden entry '* → PLAN' temporally applies post-lock (after PLAN is finalized). The allowed edges ROUTER → PLAN and ARCH → PLAN are pre-lock routing transitions that occur before PLAN is finalized, so they are not in conflict with the post-lock invariant.

states.description: Post-execution quality assurance and runtime verification
Terminal state — task marked done, execution lock released
Defect resolution — diagnosed and fixes PostVerify failures
Terminal state — unrecoverable issue, escalation to HUMAN

edges.condition: LEVEL_1, simple implementation with no planning
Gate decision: BLOCK — requires architecture review
Gate decision: FAIL after CODE retry exhausted
BLOCK — cannot fix, escalate to architecture

forbidden.reason: Single-pass execution — CODE never returns to planning
Execution complete — no re-planning after verification
No re-routing — ROUTER is single-use per session
No self-loop (except retry via POST_VERIFY → CODE)
DEBUG never delegates back to CODE — prevents delegation loops
COMMIT is terminal — no transitions out
BLOCK is terminal — no transitions out

FLAG.description: Non-blocking concerns documented; proceed to implementation

BLOCK.description: Critical issue requires architecture review before implementation

PASS.description: All runtime checks pass; static analysis clean

FLAG.description: Runtime checks pass; non-blocking static flags documented

FAIL.condition: First FAIL → CODE (max 1 retry); subsequent FAIL after CODE retry exhausted → DEBUG
FAIL.description: Runtime check failed or blocking static issue

BLOCK.description: Security issue, architecture violation, or repeated FAIL beyond retry limit

execution_lock.activation: Lock activates on PLAN → PRE_VERIFY transition (first transition OUT of planning)

schema.state: IDLE | ROUTING | PLANNING | LOCKED | EXECUTING | VERIFYING | COMMITTED | BLOCKED
schema.phase: IDLE | ROUTING | PLANNING | ARCH_REVIEWING | PRE_VERIFYING | EXECUTING | POST_VERIFYING | COMMITTED | BLOCKED
schema.current_agent: router | plan | architect | code | debug | pre_verify | post_verify | null

escalation_rules.action: Route to ARCH for architecture review
Route to ARCH for architecture review
Escalate to HUMAN — guard violation is critical
Escalate to HUMAN for binding resolution

retry_limits.on_exhaust: Route to DEBUG (or ARCH BLOCK if DEBUG also fails)
N/A — escalation is the only outcome
Route to POST_VERIFY FAIL (treated as CODE retry exhausted → DEBUG)
